message:
feat(fnp-python): *_like family handles F-contig source natively (br-franken_numpy-hsw9)

Sub-bead 2 of F-contig decomposition.

native_like_array no longer bails for F-contiguous multi-D source with
order='K'. Instead it sets an emit_fortran flag when the source reports
F_CONTIGUOUS && !C_CONTIGUOUS (i.e. strictly column-major, 2-D+), then
routes the final export through build_numpy_array_from_ufunc_fortran so
the output's F_CONTIGUOUS flag matches numpy's asfortranarray-backed
baseline in zeros_like / ones_like / empty_like / full_like.

This closes the F-layout parity hole left by sub-bead 1: the four
*_like parity tests all exercise an F-source default-order='K' case
(`np.asfortranarray(...)` input with no explicit order), and previously
that path hit the numpy fallback. Now it stays native end-to-end.

All four parity tests pass on rch:
  zeros_like_matches_numpy_across_layout_dtype_shape_object_scalar_and_zero
  ones_like_matches_numpy_across_layout_dtype_shape_object_scalar_and_zero
  empty_like_matches_numpy_across_surface_dtype_shape_scalar_and_zero
  full_like_matches_numpy_across_layout_dtype_shape_subok_object_and_zero

Full suite: 395/395 lib tests green.
